topic: Someone finds out their best friend is moving across the country
raw_output:
[story 1]

The postcard, tucked beneath a stack of forgotten textbooks, felt heavy and cold in my palm. The address, stark and unfamiliar, was a city so far away it felt like another planet. My stomach clenched, a tight, unpleasant knot forming beneath my ribs. Sarah. My Sarah. She was leaving. The silence of my dorm room suddenly amplified, every ticking clock sounding like a countdown to a void. I traced the embossed lettering on the card, the paper rough against my fingertips, unable to pull my gaze away from the stark reality of her departure. A tremor ran through my hand, making the card wobble precariously.

[story 2]

I stared at the screen, the text blurring into meaningless shapes. It was a confirmation email, the final word on the news that had been circling in my head like a persistent, unwelcome shadow. Moving. Across the country. My breath hitched, catching somewhere in my throat. I stood up too quickly, knocking my chair backward with a jarring scrape against the floor. My legs felt unsteady, as if gravity had suddenly shifted. I squeezed my eyes shut for a moment, trying to steady the frantic rhythm of my pulse, before forcing myself to look at the words again, the sheer distance between us suddenly immense and insurmountable.

[story 3]

When Liam finally told me, his voice was unnervingly even, devoid of the usual easy warmth. The news settled over me like a thick, suffocating blanket. I didn't move for a long time, just sat on the edge of the bed, my knuckles white where I gripped the worn fabric of my jeans. The air in the room seemed too thin to draw in. Every sound—the distant traffic, the hum of the refrigerator—felt magnified, pressing in on my skin. I kept replaying the conversation in my mind, searching for a flaw, a misunderstanding, anything to justify this seismic shift in our friendship, but there was nothing solid to hold onto.
